title: Receive offers - Handle offers | CanadaBuys

breadcrumbs.title: Apply the phased offer compliance process
Determine if a peer review is required

structure_blocks.heading: Handle offers
structure_blocks.text: Handle offers

structure_blocks.heading: Handle offers
structure_blocks.text: You should use a detailed and systematic approach when managing and evaluating offers within federal procurement. This ensures the process is fair, compliant, and maintains the integrity of procurement activities. Below are the procedural guidelines designed to assist you from the moment offers are received to contract award.

structure_blocks.heading: Initial preparation and receipt
structure_blocks.text: Initial preparation and receipt

structure_blocks.heading: Initial preparation and receipt
structure_blocks.text: This section provides guidelines on how to properly receive and log offers, ensuring they are securely stored and accurately recorded.

structure_blocks.heading: Offers reception
structure_blocks.text: Offers reception

structure_blocks.heading: Offers reception
structure_blocks.text: Upon receipt, the responsible staff will log each offer, date, and time-stamp ensuring that electronic submissions are saved and that paper submissions remain unopened in a secure locked location until after the solicitation of offers closing date and time.

structure_blocks.heading: Identification of offers received
structure_blocks.text: Identification of offers received

structure_blocks.heading: Identification of offers received
structure_blocks.text: For physical packages lacking clear identification such as the solicitation number, offeror’s name, return address and solicitation closing date and time, the responsible staff will open them solely for the purpose of identifying essential details. They will then reseal, label with the pertinent information, initial and store them securely.

structure_blocks.heading: Paper offers
structure_blocks.text: Paper offers

structure_blocks.heading: Paper offers
structure_blocks.text: Paper version offers received after the solicitation closing date and time, including any from solicitations that have been cancelled, are returned to the offerors unopened and the electronic versions will be deleted. A covering letter explaining the reason why the late offer (paper and electronic) is not being considered will be sent to the offeror by the offer reception staff.

structure_blocks.heading: Paper offers
structure_blocks.text: If the envelope or package does not contain sufficient information to identify the offeror and/or the solicitation number, it will be opened for identification purposes and returned to the offeror with the appropriate letter explaining the reason for opening the offer.

structure_blocks.heading: Paper offers
structure_blocks.text: For Public Services and Procurement Canada (PSPC) in the National Capital Region (NCR), offers received by the mailroom are time and date stamped and delivered unopened to the Offer Receiving Unit (ORU).

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: Modify and withdraw offers

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: Here are some key guidelines for handling offer modifications and withdrawals:

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: if permitted by the solicitation terms and an offeror raises its price before the closing, any necessary additional financial security must be provided within a reasonable timeframe, typically within 5 business days

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: an offer withdrawn post-closing cannot be resubmitted

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: offers accompanied by offer security may be withdrawn without financial penalty to the offeror if a significant error is identified within the offer

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: an error such as missing information, for example, must be approved as significant at the director level

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: when an offeror intends to withdraw an offer for reasons other than significant errors, you must consult with legal services

structure_blocks.heading: Modify and withdraw offers
structure_blocks.text: if PSPC permits the withdrawal of an offer submitted with offer security due to a significant error and there was a public opening, an advisory notice signed by a director must be issued to all offerors

structure_blocks.heading: Secure handling of offers
structure_blocks.text: Secure handling of offers

structure_blocks.heading: Secure handling of offers
structure_blocks.text: The offers receiving staff will follow the instructions you give regarding the security of the offers.

structure_blocks.heading: Secure handling of offers
structure_blocks.text: For offers marked protected, confidential, secret, or top secret, refer to the Contract Security Program’s website and follow government procedures for the transmittal of protected or classified information or assets. These offers must be delivered directly to you, as the buyer, and receipted to ensure adherence to security protocols.

structure_blocks.heading: Special handling considerations
structure_blocks.text: Special handling considerations

structure_blocks.heading: Special handling considerations
structure_blocks.text: You, as the buyer, must contact your receiving unit before solicitation closing date when an alternative receiving location is required (for example, a large amount or bulky offers are expected). The receiving personnel, in consultation with departmental security, is responsible for the assessment of the alternative location’s security and ensuring the complete physical security of offers from receipt to opening. The personnel of the offer receiving location are responsible for recording submissions received at these locations.

structure_blocks.heading: Receipt of quotations
structure_blocks.text: Receipt of quotations

structure_blocks.heading: Receipt of quotations
structure_blocks.text: Written offers (quotations) submitted in response to a Request for Quotations, which are sent directly to you, will be declared non-responsive if received after the closing date and time regardless of the date of mailing.

structure_blocks.heading: Receipt of quotations
structure_blocks.text: To ensure that all responsive quotations are considered and to accommodate internal mail delivery schedules, you may need to delay the award of a purchase order until after delivery of the first morning mail on the day following the closing date.

structure_blocks.heading: Receipt of quotations
structure_blocks.text: You must sign and date quotations upon receipt. Sectors/regions must ensure that the receipt, custody, and handling of quotations submitted directly to you are conducted in a manner that reflects the principle of fairness to all offerors.

structure_blocks.heading: Receipt of quotations
structure_blocks.text: For quotations sent directly to you, no modifications will be accepted after the initial offer unless they are performed through negotiation processes facilitated by PSPC and involve all offerors who initially submitted compliant offers.

structure_blocks.heading: Telephone-solicited offers
structure_blocks.text: Telephone-solicited offers

structure_blocks.heading: Telephone-solicited offers
structure_blocks.text: You must accurately transcribe details of each offer solicited via telephone, immediately noting the date, time, and their initials on file for accuracy and accountability.

structure_blocks.heading: Secure opening
structure_blocks.text: Secure opening

structure_blocks.heading: Secure opening
structure_blocks.text: Securing the opening of offer information is essential to protect confidentiality and fairness during the procurement process.

structure_blocks.heading: Opening of offers
structure_blocks.text: Opening of offers

structure_blocks.heading: Opening of offers
structure_blocks.text: After the solicitation closing date and time, authorized staff will open all paper and electronic offers in the presence of at least one witness to uphold transparency and integrity.

structure_blocks.heading: Completeness and audit preparation
structure_blocks.text: Completeness and audit preparation

structure_blocks.heading: Completeness and audit preparation
structure_blocks.text: The specified authorized staff will screen each offer for completeness. They will also make copies of any corrections or annotations for retention and audit purposes, and ensure that no alterations are possible.

structure_blocks.heading: Completeness and audit preparation
structure_blocks.text: As evidence that paper documents were processed and verified, the receiving unit perforates financial security documents and stamps the front page of each technical documentation volume. All the offers are then verified and certified against the source list which is kept on the procurement file.

structure_blocks.heading: Public opening procedures
structure_blocks.text: Public opening procedures

structure_blocks.heading: Public opening procedures
structure_blocks.text: When offers are opened publicly, the paper version offers are removed from the secure locked location and transported to the place of public opening. All offers (paper and electronic versions) received will be opened in the presence of a witness. The name and address of each offeror and the amount of each offer are read out.

structure_blocks.heading: Public opening procedures
structure_blocks.text: If there are multi-items listed in the offer but there is no total offer price, the offer price on each item is read out. It is also confirmed that offer security (if required) is included in the offer.

structure_blocks.heading: Confidentiality of offers
structure_blocks.text: Confidentiality of offers

structure_blocks.heading: Confidentiality of offers
structure_blocks.text: Ensuring the confidentiality of offer information is critical for maintaining the integrity of the procurement process.

structure_blocks.heading: Secure handling of offer information
structure_blocks.text: Secure handling of offer information

structure_blocks.heading: Secure handling of offer information
structure_blocks.text: You must treat all information in a secure and confidential manner to ensure the integrity of the contracting process.

structure_blocks.heading: Sharing with the offer evaluation team
heading_path: Sharing with the offer evaluation team
structure_blocks.text: Sharing with the offer evaluation team

structure_blocks.heading: Sharing with the offer evaluation team
heading_path: Sharing with the offer evaluation team
structure_blocks.text: When referring offers to the client (or technical evaluators) during the evaluation process, the following cautionary note must be provided to the client: "Offer information must be divulged only to individuals authorized to participate in this contracting process. Information must not be divulged to, or discussed with the private industry."

structure_blocks.heading: Response to inquiries about offeror identities
heading_path: Response to inquiries about offeror identities
structure_blocks.text: Response to inquiries about offeror identities

structure_blocks.heading: Response to inquiries about offeror identities
heading_path: Response to inquiries about offeror identities
structure_blocks.text: From offer closing to contract award, including the contract approval phase, you are prohibited from disclosing offerors’ names in response to inquiries. Businesses seeking information should be directed to the Access to Information and Privacy Office .

structure_blocks.heading: Response to inquiries about offeror identities
heading_path: Response to inquiries about offeror identities
structure_blocks.text: After contract award, the release of offerors’ names and other pertinent information may be disclosed in accordance with departmental policy.

structure_blocks.heading: Managing the offer validity period
structure_blocks.text: Managing the offer validity period

structure_blocks.heading: Managing the offer validity period
structure_blocks.text: The period during which offers remain valid for acceptance is crucial for the integrity and efficiency of the procurement process. You can manage this period effectively by following the guidelines below.

structure_blocks.heading: Standard validity periods
structure_blocks.text: Standard validity periods

structure_blocks.heading: Standard validity periods
structure_blocks.text: Offers will remain open for acceptance for a period of 60 days (30 days for construction) from the solicitation closing date unless specified otherwise in the solicitation. These timeframes ensure that both you and the offerors have a clear understanding of the duration for which offers must be honored.

structure_blocks.heading: Monitoring and adjustment
structure_blocks.text: Monitoring and adjustment

structure_blocks.heading: Monitoring and adjustment
structure_blocks.text: You must carefully assess the potential for extended offer evaluation periods and indicate in the solicitation of offers the modified period for offer acceptance. You must also closely monitor the offer evaluation and contract approval processes and plan accordingly to ensure contracts are awarded within the offer validity period. Expiry of offer acceptance periods before contract award should become an exception.

structure_blocks.heading: Action upon expiry of the offer validity period
heading_path: Action upon expiry of the offer validity period
structure_blocks.text: Action upon expiry of the offer validity period

structure_blocks.heading: Action upon expiry of the offer validity period
heading_path: Action upon expiry of the offer validity period
structure_blocks.text: If the offer acceptance period expires prior to contract award, the solicitation of offers must be reissued.

structure_blocks.heading: Contracts awarded post-expiry
structure_blocks.text: Contracts awarded post-expiry

structure_blocks.heading: Contracts awarded post-expiry
structure_blocks.text: Any contract awarded to an offeror after the offer expiry date is considered a sole-source contract and must be justified accordingly.

structure_blocks.heading: Incomplete evaluations
structure_blocks.text: Incomplete evaluations

structure_blocks.heading: Incomplete evaluations
structure_blocks.text: If the evaluation of offers is incomplete and is unlikely to be completed before the offer acceptance period expires, it's essential to halt the process. Assess the reasons for the delay and make any necessary adjustments to the solicitation or evaluation methodology, then reissue the solicitation of offers.

structure_blocks.heading: Extending the offer validity period
structure_blocks.text: Extending the offer validity period

structure_blocks.heading: Extending the offer validity period
structure_blocks.text: The Government of Canada may request in writing an extension of the offer validity period from all responsive offerors a minimum of three days before the end of the offer validity period. If all responsive offerors accept the extension, we will continue with the evaluation of the offers under the extended period.

structure_blocks.heading: Extending the offer validity period
structure_blocks.text: If all responsive offerors do not accept the extension, we will either continue with the evaluation of the offers of those who accepted the extension or cancel the solicitation.

structure_blocks.heading: Handling non-agreement for extensions
structure_blocks.text: Handling non-agreement for extensions

structure_blocks.heading: Handling non-agreement for extensions
structure_blocks.text: In cases where an offeror does not agree to the extension and it is clear that the offeror has no chance of being recommended for award, it may be appropriate to exercise our right to proceed with the evaluation of only those offers submitted by the offerors that have agreed to an extension.

structure_blocks.heading: Handling non-agreement for extensions
structure_blocks.text: Legal Services may be consulted in instances where an offeror does not agree to the extension, particularly in the case of procurements subject to trade agreements.

raw_text:
Handle offers

You should use a detailed and systematic approach when managing and evaluating offers within federal procurement. This ensures the process is fair, compliant, and maintains the integrity of procurement activities. Below are the procedural guidelines designed to assist you from the moment offers are received to contract award.

Initial preparation and receipt

This section provides guidelines on how to properly receive and log offers, ensuring they are securely stored and accurately recorded.

Offers reception

Upon receipt, the responsible staff will log each offer, date, and time-stamp ensuring that electronic submissions are saved and that paper submissions remain unopened in a secure locked location until after the solicitation of offers closing date and time.

Identification of offers received

For physical packages lacking clear identification such as the solicitation number, offeror’s name, return address and solicitation closing date and time, the responsible staff will open them solely for the purpose of identifying essential details. They will then reseal, label with the pertinent information, initial and store them securely.

Paper offers

Paper version offers received after the solicitation closing date and time, including any from solicitations that have been cancelled, are returned to the offerors unopened and the electronic versions will be deleted. A covering letter explaining the reason why the late offer (paper and electronic) is not being considered will be sent to the offeror by the offer reception staff.

If the envelope or package does not contain sufficient information to identify the offeror and/or the solicitation number, it will be opened for identification purposes and returned to the offeror with the appropriate letter explaining the reason for opening the offer.

For Public Services and Procurement Canada (PSPC) in the National Capital Region (NCR), offers received by the mailroom are time and date stamped and delivered unopened to the Offer Receiving Unit (ORU).

Modify and withdraw offers

Here are some key guidelines for handling offer modifications and withdrawals:

if permitted by the solicitation terms and an offeror raises its price before the closing, any necessary additional financial security must be provided within a reasonable timeframe, typically within 5 business days

an offer withdrawn post-closing cannot be resubmitted

offers accompanied by offer security may be withdrawn without financial penalty to the offeror if a significant error is identified within the offer

an error such as missing information, for example, must be approved as significant at the director level

when an offeror intends to withdraw an offer for reasons other than significant errors, you must consult with legal services

if PSPC permits the withdrawal of an offer submitted with offer security due to a significant error and there was a public opening, an advisory notice signed by a director must be issued to all offerors

Secure handling of offers

The offers receiving staff will follow the instructions you give regarding the security of the offers.

For offers marked protected, confidential, secret, or top secret, refer to the Contract Security Program’s website and follow government procedures for the transmittal of protected or classified information or assets. These offers must be delivered directly to you, as the buyer, and receipted to ensure adherence to security protocols.

Special handling considerations

You, as the buyer, must contact your receiving unit before solicitation closing date when an alternative receiving location is required (for example, a large amount or bulky offers are expected). The receiving personnel, in consultation with departmental security, is responsible for the assessment of the alternative location’s security and ensuring the complete physical security of offers from receipt to opening. The personnel of the offer receiving location are responsible for recording submissions received at these locations.

Receipt of quotations

Written offers (quotations) submitted in response to a Request for Quotations, which are sent directly to you, will be declared non-responsive if received after the closing date and time regardless of the date of mailing.

To ensure that all responsive quotations are considered and to accommodate internal mail delivery schedules, you may need to delay the award of a purchase order until after delivery of the first morning mail on the day following the closing date.

You must sign and date quotations upon receipt. Sectors/regions must ensure that the receipt, custody, and handling of quotations submitted directly to you are conducted in a manner that reflects the principle of fairness to all offerors.

For quotations sent directly to you, no modifications will be accepted after the initial offer unless they are performed through negotiation processes facilitated by PSPC and involve all offerors who initially submitted compliant offers.

Telephone-solicited offers

You must accurately transcribe details of each offer solicited via telephone, immediately noting the date, time, and their initials on file for accuracy and accountability.

Secure opening

Securing the opening of offer information is essential to protect confidentiality and fairness during the procurement process.

Opening of offers

After the solicitation closing date and time, authorized staff will open all paper and electronic offers in the presence of at least one witness to uphold transparency and integrity.

Completeness and audit preparation

The specified authorized staff will screen each offer for completeness. They will also make copies of any corrections or annotations for retention and audit purposes, and ensure that no alterations are possible.

As evidence that paper documents were processed and verified, the receiving unit perforates financial security documents and stamps the front page of each technical documentation volume. All the offers are then verified and certified against the source list which is kept on the procurement file.

Public opening procedures

When offers are opened publicly, the paper version offers are removed from the secure locked location and transported to the place of public opening. All offers (paper and electronic versions) received will be opened in the presence of a witness. The name and address of each offeror and the amount of each offer are read out.

If there are multi-items listed in the offer but there is no total offer price, the offer price on each item is read out. It is also confirmed that offer security (if required) is included in the offer.

Confidentiality of offers

Ensuring the confidentiality of offer information is critical for maintaining the integrity of the procurement process.

Secure handling of offer information

You must treat all information in a secure and confidential manner to ensure the integrity of the contracting process.

Sharing with the offer evaluation team

When referring offers to the client (or technical evaluators) during the evaluation process, the following cautionary note must be provided to the client: "Offer information must be divulged only to individuals authorized to participate in this contracting process. Information must not be divulged to, or discussed with the private industry."

Response to inquiries about offeror identities

From offer closing to contract award, including the contract approval phase, you are prohibited from disclosing offerors’ names in response to inquiries. Businesses seeking information should be directed to the Access to Information and Privacy Office .

After contract award, the release of offerors’ names and other pertinent information may be disclosed in accordance with departmental policy.

Managing the offer validity period

The period during which offers remain valid for acceptance is crucial for the integrity and efficiency of the procurement process. You can manage this period effectively by following the guidelines below.

Standard validity periods

Offers will remain open for acceptance for a period of 60 days (30 days for construction) from the solicitation closing date unless specified otherwise in the solicitation. These timeframes ensure that both you and the offerors have a clear understanding of the duration for which offers must be honored.

Monitoring and adjustment

You must carefully assess the potential for extended offer evaluation periods and indicate in the solicitation of offers the modified period for offer acceptance. You must also closely monitor the offer evaluation and contract approval processes and plan accordingly to ensure contracts are awarded within the offer validity period. Expiry of offer acceptance periods before contract award should become an exception.

Action upon expiry of the offer validity period

If the offer acceptance period expires prior to contract award, the solicitation of offers must be reissued.

Contracts awarded post-expiry

Any contract awarded to an offeror after the offer expiry date is considered a sole-source contract and must be justified accordingly.

Incomplete evaluations

If the evaluation of offers is incomplete and is unlikely to be completed before the offer acceptance period expires, it's essential to halt the process. Assess the reasons for the delay and make any necessary adjustments to the solicitation or evaluation methodology, then reissue the solicitation of offers.

Extending the offer validity period

The Government of Canada may request in writing an extension of the offer validity period from all responsive offerors a minimum of three days before the end of the offer validity period. If all responsive offerors accept the extension, we will continue with the evaluation of the offers under the extended period.

If all responsive offerors do not accept the extension, we will either continue with the evaluation of the offers of those who accepted the extension or cancel the solicitation.

Handling non-agreement for extensions

In cases where an offeror does not agree to the extension and it is clear that the offeror has no chance of being recommended for award, it may be appropriate to exercise our right to proceed with the evaluation of only those offers submitted by the offerors that have agreed to an extension.

Legal Services may be consulted in instances where an offeror does not agree to the extension, particularly in the case of procurements subject to trade agreements.

Handle offers

You should use a detailed and systematic approach when managing and evaluating offers within federal procurement. This ensures the process is fair, compliant, and maintains the integrity of procurement activities. Below are the procedural guidelines designed to assist you from the moment offers are received to contract award.

Initial preparation and receipt

This section provides guidelines on how to properly receive and log offers, ensuring they are securely stored and accurately recorded.
